prose: Fenofibrate is returned as a negative call at the adversarial_negative tier, and the reasoning rests on the absence of curated mechanism rather than the presence of activity. There is no curated Parkinson's DirectEvidence and no registration as a neuro-AOP stressor [E1], which is the only admissible basis for a positive evidence-ranked mechanistic hypothesis here. The compound is genuinely bioactive, with four mechanistic assay hits including two mitochondrial [E2], but this is treated strictly as anti-diagnostic corroboration—the imposter signal the engine deliberately does not act upon, since bioactivity alone cannot establish a disease-relevant mechanism. Two orthogonal strands reinforce implausibility rather than drive the call: real-world pharmacovigilance shows no parkinsonism signal across 35,322 FAERS reports (ROR 0.9) [E3], and structure-based prediction indicates low, BBB-negative brain exposure, a pharmacokinetic reason it is unlikely to reach relevant targets [E4]. Correctly not flagged.

citations.detail: No curated Parkinson's DirectEvidence and not a registered neuro-AOP stressor.
Bioactive: 4 mechanistic assay hits (incl. 2 mitochondrial). This is the imposter signal the engine correctly does NOT act on.
Zero parkinsonism signal across 35322 FAERS reports (ROR 0.9, not elevated).
Low brain exposure (BBB-negative): an orthogonal pharmacokinetic reason it cannot be a driver.
